paragraph: Single-family homes dominate the Fair Lawn market with 107 sales over six months at a median of $730,000, and this is a real seller's market. Homes are selling 2.9 percent above list price on average, with nearly 63 percent of sales closing above asking, and the median time on market sits at 19 days. Sellers are dictating terms here. The range runs from $427,000 to $1,467,300, so there is breadth at every price point, but inventory moves quickly once it hits the market. Condos and townhouses represent a much smaller slice of Fair Lawn activity at 12 sales in the same period with a median of $370,025. They're moving fast too, at a median of 15.5 days on market and 75 percent selling above asking, though the sample is small enough that any single sale shifts the picture. If you're weighing single-family versus condo in Fair Lawn, the deciding factor isn't market heat. Both tiers are competitive right now. It's whether you want the space and grounds of a single-family home or the low-maintenance lifestyle and lower price point of a condo.
